assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 30: 'Concurrent triage detected on the same upstream component (quinn-proto)' and lines 24-26 show TC-8019 with status 'In Progress' in the search results table.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 32: 'TC-8019 is currently **In Progress** and assigned to **engineer-b@example.com**.' Both the issue key and assignee are explicitly present.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 38-43: Three options are listed: (1) Wait -- pause triage until TC-8019 completes, (2) Skip -- skip remediation task creation entirely, (3) Proceed -- create tasks with 'concurrent-triage-overlap' label. All three match the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 46: 'This check runs **before** Case A/B/C branching in Step 8.' Also triage-outcome.md line 11: 'This concurrent triage warning was presented to the engineer before proceeding to Step 8 (Case A/B/C branching).'
